role_delineation_check: The Social Worker manages the clinical discharge plan and home health agency selection, while the Navigator maintains the patient record in Atlantis, verifies demographic accuracy, and provides education on the Healing Partners program and Medical Assistant roles to ensure a smooth transition for the family.
narrative_summary: Arthur Miller, an 86-year-old gentleman recovering from a hip fracture following a fall complicated by dementia, experienced a delay in his transition home when the Navigator was excluded from a critical planning session. This communication gap meant the family was left without clarity on the start of home health services, leading to increased caregiver stress. The Navigator bridged this divide by requesting a direct update from the Social Worker and then meeting with Arthur's daughter to clarify how the Healing Partners program supports the transition process, ensuring the family felt prepared for the Medical Assistant's first visit.
demographic_audit_note: I performed a thorough audit of the facility face sheet against the Atlantis record and identified a discrepancy in the apartment number for Arthur's residence; I corrected this in Atlantis to ensure the home health agency and Medical Assistant have the accurate location for the first visit.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with Home Health Agency support
v_card_flyer_status: During my visit with Arthur's daughter, Eleanor, I explained the importance of recognizing our calls and successfully saved the Healing Partners V-Card into her phone contacts while providing a program flyer for her to review.
pre_dc_pulse_call: I spoke with Eleanor 24 hours before the scheduled discharge; she confirmed Arthur's readiness to return home but expressed lingering concerns about his safety during the night, which I documented as a sentiment observation in Atlantis.
atlantis_final_sync: After the Social Worker verbally confirmed that the home health agency had accepted the case and set a start date, I documented the discharge date and the scheduled first visit for the Medical Assistant in Atlantis.

format_1_state_log.event_description: I met with Arthur and his daughter to introduce the Healing Partners program; they expressed interest, and I documented their contact information and Arthur's history of wandering in Atlantis.

The daughter informed me during a hallway conversation that the discharge plan had changed to an earlier date during a morning huddle I was not invited to; I noted this communication gap and the new potential date in Atlantis.

I asked the Social Worker for a verbal status update on the new discharge timeline and the assigned home health agency; the Social Worker confirmed the agency but noted they were still waiting for internal acceptance.

The Social Worker notified me that the home health agency is now in place for a Thursday discharge; I updated Atlantis, confirmed the time with the daughter, and scheduled the Medical Assistant for the Friday morning visit.

format_2_triples.situation: The family is unclear about the sequence of events on the first day Arthur is back at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator explained the role of the Medical Assistant and the 24-hour post-discharge visit window to set clear expectations for the first day of care.

format_2_triples.situation: The Navigator was not included in the weekly utilization review where the patient's discharge date was moved up.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator requested a verbal summary of the meeting from the Social Worker and documented the updated timeline in Atlantis for tracking.

format_2_triples.situation: Arthur's daughter asks if the home health nurse can help with Arthur's specific dementia medications on the first night.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator informed the daughter that clinical care and medication management questions should be directed to the Social Worker and the nursing team.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator notices that the Social Worker has not yet provided the name of the assigned home health agency despite the discharge being only two days away. The Navigator documents the current lack of information and the date in Atlantis and waits for the Social Worker to provide the update.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's ownership of the discharge process and avoids premature coordination or vendor contact.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator approaches the Social Worker to ask for a status update on the home health referral and confirms the daughter's readiness for the transition. The Navigator then updates Arthur's demographic information in Atlantis to ensure no delays occur once the agency is confirmed.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This action remains within the Navigator's scope by seeking information from the appropriate internal channel and maintaining data accuracy.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency's intake coordinator directly to see if they have received Arthur's referral and to ask for the name of the nurse who will be visiting. The Navigator then tells the daughter the exact time the nurse will arrive to ease her anxiety.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the no-vendor rule and involves the Navigator in clinical scheduling and logistics that are the responsibility of the Social Worker and the agency.
